content.text: Multi-Tenant Management

Azure Lighthouse multi-tenant management transforms the managing tenant's Azure portal into a control plane that spans every delegated customer tenant simultaneously. After delegations are established, the MSP uses a single set of Azure tools — Resource Graph, Azure Policy, Azure Monitor, Defender for Cloud, Advisor — across all customers without switching directories. The 'My Customers' blade in the portal shows all delegation assignments; the MSP can filter, sort, and drill into any customer subscription from there.

attrs.alt: Azure Lighthouse multi-tenant — MSP managing hub and four customer tenant delegations, single-pane operations

content.text: Multi-tenant Resource Graph queries are limited to delegated subscriptions that appear in the MSP's context. If a customer subscription was delegated at resource group scope only, that subscription does not appear in cross-subscription Resource Graph queries — only the specific resource groups do.
MSP Operations at Scale
Operation
MSP Tool
What Changes Across Tenants
Inventory query
Resource Graph (cross-subscription)
Queries span all delegated customer subscriptions
Policy assignment
az policy assignment create --subscription
Policy assigned in customer scope, evaluated there
Policy compliance
az policy state list --subscription
Customer's compliance state, read from MSP context
VM patching
Azure Update Manager
Patch schedules and assessments in customer subscriptions
Alert rules
az monitor metrics alert create --subscription
Alert created in customer sub, fires to customer LA
Workbook / Dashboard
Azure Monitor Workbook
Source is customer LA workspace; workbook runs in MSP portal
Defender CSPM
Defender for Cloud overview
Recommendations from all customer tenants consolidated
Cross-Tenant Monitoring with Azure Monitor

An MSP can create Azure Monitor alert rules and workbooks that reference Log Analytics workspaces in customer tenants. The workspace resource ID is in the customer tenant, but the MSP engineer queries it using their Lighthouse-delegated Monitoring Reader role. Alert notification actions (email, webhook) are configured in the MSP tenant.

# --- Create an alert rule in a customer subscription (from MSP tenant) ---
# The alert is created IN the customer subscription; notifications go to MSP action groups

# Step 1: get the customer Log Analytics workspace ID
CUSTOMER_LA_ID=$(az monitor log-analytics workspace show   --resource-group monitoring-rg   --workspace-name customer-law   --subscription $CUSTOMER_SUB_ID   --query id -o tsv)

# Step 2: create a scheduled query alert for high CPU in the customer sub
az monitor scheduled-query create   --resource-group monitoring-rg   --name "high-cpu-alert"   --subscription $CUSTOMER_SUB_ID   --scopes $CUSTOMER_LA_ID   --condition-query "Perf | where ObjectName=='Processor' and CounterName=='% Processor Time' | summarize avg(CounterValue) by Computer | where avg_CounterValue > 90"   --condition-time-aggregation Average   --condition-threshold 90   --condition-operator GreaterThan   --evaluation-frequency PT5M   --window-size PT15M   --severity 2   --action-groups $MSP_ACTION_GROUP_ID  # <-- MSP action group in MSP tenant

# Step 3: query across all customer workspaces from MSP tenant (union)
# In Log Analytics: use workspace() function to span multiple customer workspaces
# union withsource=workspace *
# | where $ws in ("customer-a-law", "customer-b-law", "customer-c-law")
# | where TimeGenerated > ago(1h)
# | where ObjectName == "Processor" and CounterValue > 80
# | summarize avg(CounterValue) by Computer, workspace

# Cross-tenant Azure Monitor alert list (from MSP portal)
az monitor alert list   --subscription $CUSTOMER_SUB_ID   --query "[?properties.essentials.severity=='Sev2'].{name:name, state:properties.essentials.alertState}"   -o table
Defender for Cloud in Multi-Tenant Context

Microsoft Defender for Cloud shows a consolidated security posture for all delegated subscriptions when accessed from the managing tenant. Security recommendations and alerts from customer tenants appear in the MSP's Defender for Cloud overview. The MSP can remediate recommendations and enable Defender plans in customer subscriptions if they hold the required role (Security Admin).

# List Defender for Cloud alerts from a customer subscription (MSP context)
az security alert list   --subscription $CUSTOMER_SUB_ID   --query "[?properties.status=='Active'].{name:name, severity:properties.severity,             description:properties.description, resource:properties.compromisedEntity}"   -o table | head -20

# List CSPM security recommendations for a customer subscription
az security assessment list   --subscription $CUSTOMER_SUB_ID   --query "[?properties.status.code=='Unhealthy'].{name:displayName,             severity:properties.metadata.severity,             resource:properties.resourceDetails.Id}"   -o table | head -20

# Enable Defender for Servers Plan 2 in a customer subscription (requires Security Admin)
az security pricing create   --name "VirtualMachines"   --tier "Standard"   --subscription $CUSTOMER_SUB_ID

# Cross-customer secure score comparison (from MSP tenant, multiple customer subs)
for SUB in $CUSTOMER_SUB_ID_A $CUSTOMER_SUB_ID_B $CUSTOMER_SUB_ID_C; do
  SCORE=$(az security secure-score show --name "ascScore"     --subscription "$SUB"     --query "properties.score.current" -o tsv)
  echo "Subscription $SUB: Secure Score = $SCORE"
done
Managing MSP Offer Updates and Version Control

When an MSP needs to update a delegation (add a principal, change a role, add eligible authorizations), they redeploy the ARM template to the customer's subscription with the same registration definition GUID. The update is in-place: the registrationAssignment does not change; only the definition properties update. This means customers do not need to re-accept the offer.

# --- MSP: update authorizations in the delegation template ---
# Add a new eligible authorization for a security group
# Edit lighthouse-delegation.json: add new object to eligibleAuthorizations[]

# Redeploy to customer subscription (same template = in-place update)
az deployment sub create   --subscription $CUSTOMER_SUB_ID   --location eastus   --template-file ./lighthouse-delegation-v2.json   --parameters mspTenantId=$MSP_TENANT_ID mspOfferName="Contoso MSP v2"

# --- Marketplace offer update path ---
# 1. Update the authorizations JSON in Partner Center offer
# 2. Submit offer for review (Microsoft takes ~3 business days)
# 3. After approval, customers who accepted the offer see an "Update available" notification
#    in their Azure portal → Service Providers blade
# 4. Customer clicks "Update" — deploys the new definition version
# 5. No downtime: access remains active during the update

# --- Audit which customers are on which offer version ---
for SUB in "${CUSTOMER_SUBS[@]}"; do
  echo "=== $SUB ==="
  az managedservices definition list --subscription "$SUB"     --query "[].{offer:properties.registrationDefinitionName,                  version:properties.description}" -o table
done

# --- Remove a single authorization (without full redeploy) ---
# Must redeploy the full template with the principal removed from authorizations[].
# Azure Lighthouse does not support partial authorization removal via PATCH.
Service Provider and Customer Views

Lighthouse provides separate portal blades for each side of the delegation. MSPs use the 'My Customers' blade in the managing tenant. Customers use the 'Service Providers' blade in their own tenant to see what has been delegated and who has access.

# --- MSP perspective: list all customer delegations visible from managing tenant ---
az managedservices definition list   --query "[].{offer:properties.registrationDefinitionName,                description:properties.description,                managedByTenant:properties.managedByTenantId}" -o table

# --- Customer perspective: audit what the MSP can access ---
# (Run in customer tenant)
az managedservices assignment list --subscription $CUSTOMER_SUB_ID   --query "[].{name:name,                provisioningState:properties.provisioningState,                definitionId:properties.registrationDefinitionId}" -o table

# Detailed view of who has what access (expand the definition)
az rest   --method GET   --url "https://management.azure.com/subscriptions/$CUSTOMER_SUB_ID/providers/Microsoft.ManagedServices/registrationAssignments?api-version=2022-10-01&\$expand=registrationDefinition" |   python3 -c "
import json, sys
data = json.load(sys.stdin)
for a in data.get('value', []):
  defn = a.get('properties', {}).get('registrationDefinition', {})
  auths = defn.get('properties', {}).get('authorizations', [])
  print('Offer:', defn.get('properties', {}).get('registrationDefinitionName'))
  for auth in auths:
    print('  Principal:', auth.get('principalIdDisplayName'), '|', auth.get('roleDefinitionId'))
"

# Customer: revoke delegation immediately (removes MSP access)
az managedservices assignment delete   --assignment $(az managedservices assignment list --subscription $CUSTOMER_SUB_ID     --query "[0].name" -o tsv)   --subscription $CUSTOMER_SUB_ID
Common Patterns and Anti-Patterns
Pattern
Use Case
Anti-Pattern to Avoid
One registration definition per offer tier
Tier-1 (read-only), Tier-2 (ops), Tier-3 (admin)
Single definition with all roles — violates least privilege
Entra ID groups in authorizations (not individual UPNs)
Add/remove engineers without redeploying
Individual user principalIds — breaks when staff changes
Eligible authorizations for Owner/Contributor
JIT escalation with PIM approval + MFA
Permanent Contributor for all engineers — excessive standing privilege
Delegation at subscription scope for full managed
Full-service managed offering
Delegation at every individual RG — harder to maintain and misses new RGs
Separate managing tenant for dev/test vs prod customers
Risk isolation between environments
All customers in the same managing tenant with no tier separation
Activity log alerts on cross-tenant resource changes
Detect unexpected MSP actions
Relying solely on MSP self-reporting for audit
Automating Cross-Tenant Operations with Service Principals

At-scale MSP automation — nightly compliance sweeps, weekly patch assessments, monthly cost report generation — uses a service principal in the managing tenant instead of a human identity. The SPN is added to the Lighthouse delegation authorizations with the minimum roles required. Automation scripts authenticate as the SPN and iterate over all delegated subscriptions.

# Create a service principal in the MSP tenant for automation
az ad sp create-for-rbac   --name "svc-lighthouse-automation"   --role "Reader"   --scopes "/subscriptions/$MSP_SUB_ID"   --query "{appId:appId, tenantId:tenant, secret:password}" -o json

# The SPN object ID (not appId) goes into the Lighthouse authorization's principalId
MSP_SPN_OID=$(az ad sp show --id <APP_ID> --query id -o tsv)
echo "Add to authorizations: principalId=$MSP_SPN_OID"

# Automation script: authenticate as MSP SPN and iterate over customer subscriptions
# Lighthouse delegations are visible per-sub via managedServices API

# Run as SPN
az login --service-principal   --tenant $MSP_TENANT_ID   --username $SPN_APP_ID   --password $SPN_SECRET

# Get all delegated subscriptions (subscriptions where managedServices/assignments exist)
DELEGATED_SUBS=$(az account list   --query "[?tenantId=='$MSP_TENANT_ID' && state=='Enabled'].id" -o tsv)

# Iterate: weekly patch assessment for all Arc-connected machines in each customer sub
for SUB in $DELEGATED_SUBS; do
  echo "=== Assessing patches in $SUB ==="

  # List Arc machines in this sub
  MACHINES=$(az connectedmachine list     --subscription "$SUB"     --query "[?properties.status=='Connected'].name" -o tsv)

  for MACHINE in $MACHINES; do
    RG=$(az connectedmachine show       --subscription "$SUB" --name "$MACHINE"       --query resourceGroup -o tsv)

    az vm assess-patches       --subscription "$SUB"       --resource-group "$RG"       --machine-name "$MACHINE"       --machine-type ConnectedVmwareVirtualMachine       --no-wait
  done
done

# Nightly compliance export: save policy state to MSP storage for reporting
REPORT_DATE=$(date +%Y-%m-%d)
for SUB in $DELEGATED_SUBS; do
  az policy state list --subscription "$SUB"     --query "[].{resource:resourceId,policy:policyDefinitionName,state:complianceState}"     -o json > "/reports/${SUB}_${REPORT_DATE}_policy.json"
done
Azure Advisor Recommendations Across Customer Tenants

Azure Advisor surfaces cost, reliability, performance, security, and operational excellence recommendations. From the managing tenant, the MSP can view Advisor recommendations across all delegated customer subscriptions and create action plans for customers. Recommendation suppression and deferral can be done from the MSP context.

# View all cost recommendations across a customer subscription
az advisor recommendation list   --subscription $CUSTOMER_SUB_ID   --category Cost   --query "[].{name:name, impact:properties.impact,                description:properties.shortDescription.problem,                savings:properties.extendedProperties.savingsAmount}"   -o table | head -20

# View all High-impact security recommendations
az advisor recommendation list   --subscription $CUSTOMER_SUB_ID   --category Security   --filter "properties/impact eq 'High'"   --query "[].{name:name, resource:properties.resourceMetadata.resourceId,                problem:properties.shortDescription.problem}"   -o table | head -20

# Suppress a recommendation for a specific resource (30-day deferral)
az advisor recommendation disable   --subscription $CUSTOMER_SUB_ID   --recommendation-name <RECOMMENDATION_NAME>   --days 30

# Cross-customer cost saving opportunities (loop over all delegated subs)
for SUB in $CUSTOMER_SUB_ID_A $CUSTOMER_SUB_ID_B $CUSTOMER_SUB_ID_C; do
  echo "=== Cost Advisor for $SUB ==="
  TOTAL=$(az advisor recommendation list --subscription "$SUB" --category Cost     --query "sum([].properties.extendedProperties.savingsAmount)" 2>/dev/null || echo "N/A")
  echo "Estimated monthly savings: $TOTAL"
done

# Generate a consolidated Advisor report using Resource Graph
# resources
# | where type == "microsoft.advisor/recommendations"
# | extend category = properties.category, impact = properties.impact
# | extend shortProblem = tostring(properties.shortDescription.problem)
# | summarize count() by category, impact
# | order by count_ desc
Azure Cost Management for Multi-Tenant Chargeback

MSPs can view and export cost data from customer subscriptions using Azure Cost Management if they hold the Cost Management Reader or Billing Reader role in the delegation. This enables building customer invoices, chargeback reports, and budget alerts from the managing tenant.

# View cost breakdown for a customer subscription (requires Cost Management Reader)
az costmanagement query   --scope "/subscriptions/$CUSTOMER_SUB_ID"   --type "Usage"   --timeframe "MonthToDate"   --dataset-granularity "Daily"   --dataset-aggregation '{"totalCost":{"name":"Cost","function":"Sum"}}'   --dataset-grouping '[{"type":"Dimension","name":"ServiceName"}]'   --query "properties.rows" -o table

# Export cost data for all customer subscriptions to MSP storage account (scheduled)
for SUB in "${CUSTOMER_SUBS[@]}"; do
  az costmanagement export create     --scope "/subscriptions/$SUB"     --name "monthly-cost-export"     --type "Usage"     --recurrence "Monthly"     --recurrence-period from="2024-04-01T00:00:00Z" to="2025-04-01T00:00:00Z"     --schedule-status "Active"     --dataset-granularity "Daily"     --storage-account-id $MSP_STORAGE_ACCOUNT_ID     --storage-container "cost-exports"     --storage-directory "$SUB"
done

# Budget alert: notify MSP if customer cost exceeds threshold
az consumption budget create   --resource-group billing-rg   --subscription $CUSTOMER_SUB_ID   --name "customer-monthly-budget"   --amount 5000   --time-grain Monthly   --start-date "2024-04-01"   --end-date "2025-04-01"   --category Cost   --notifications '[{
    "enabled": true,
    "operator": "GreaterThan",
    "threshold": 90,
    "contactEmails": ["billing@contoso-msp.com"],
    "contactRoles": ["Owner"]
  }]'
Security Baseline: What MSPs Must Protect

Lighthouse delegations carry significant trust. MSPs must apply strict security controls in their managing tenant to protect the access they hold to customer environments. A compromised MSP identity can affect all delegated customers simultaneously.

An MSP engineer's Entra ID account that is delegated as Contributor across 200 customer subscriptions is a very high-value target. Treat the managing tenant's identity plane as you would treat a privileged admin workstation — Conditional Access, phishing-resistant MFA, no standing access, and regular access reviews are non-negotiable.
Summary

Azure Lighthouse multi-tenant management transforms the managing tenant into a cross-tenant operations hub. MSPs query inventory, assign policy, view compliance, triage security alerts, and configure monitoring across all delegated customer subscriptions without switching directories. Delegation is managed through ARM registration definitions and assignments in the customer tenant — no guest accounts needed. Updates redeploy the definition in-place; customers retain full visibility and revocation control through the Service Providers blade. Best practices: use Entra ID groups in authorizations, apply eligible authorizations with PIM for privileged roles, delegate at subscription scope for full-service offerings, and monitor the customer Activity Log for all MSP-initiated actions.
